signin.title: Sign in to your team
signin.lead: Authentication is handled by your identity provider. You'll be redirected, sign in there, and land back here.
signin.guarantee: Your private memory is never shared and never visible in this console — that's a guarantee enforced by the backend, not by configuration.

appError.title: Backend temporarily unreachable
appError.body: The console couldn't load this page. This is usually a brief hiccup — give it a moment and retry. If it persists, the backend may be down.

connector.title: Connect your AI client
connector.lead: Paste these into the assistant's MCP configuration. The client reads and writes shared memory through this endpoint, authenticated as the team.
connector.noSecret: None — public client (PKCE). Leave the secret field empty.
connector.reachBody: Only the shared scopes you see here — global and the project scopes. Each member's private memory is never exposed through this connector.
connector.step1: Add the endpoint to your client's MCP servers.
connector.step2Secret: Authenticate with the client ID and secret.
connector.step2Public: Enter the client ID; leave the secret empty (public client, PKCE).
connector.step3: The assistant reads global first, then the active project scope.

guarantee.title: Private memory stays private
guarantee.body: Every member has a private scope the assistant uses for them alone. It is owned by the member and is never surfaced on this dashboard, in the scope browser, or through the connector above.

assistant.title: Prime your assistant
assistant.lead: Pick the scope you're working in and paste this into your Claude or ChatGPT project instructions — the assistant loads that scope's conventions from the first turn. Switch projects later? Re-copy and change only the slug line.
assistant.prompt:
You have access to kumbuka — our team's shared memory — through its MCP connector.

At the start of each session, call memory_load_context with scope "{slug}" and follow the conventions it returns — they define how this team uses kumbuka.

You are not a gatekeeper over content: the user alone decides WHAT is stored — you decide at most WHERE, and only by asking, never by refusing. Never infer a purpose from scope names.

profile.title: Display name
profile.desc: How your name appears next to entries you author in this console.
profile.hint: Your email and login credentials are managed by your identity provider — use the link above to change them.

security.title: Sign-in & credentials
security.desc: Password, two-factor authentication, and passkeys are managed in your identity provider's account console.
security.password_sub: Change your password or set up recovery options.
security.twofa_sub: Authenticator app or one-time codes by email.
security.passkey_sub: Sign in with a hardware key or platform authenticator.

connections.title: Where you're signed in
connections.desc: Every active connection to Kumbuka — this console and any assistant connector. End any you don't recognise; that session's access is revoked immediately.
connections.loadError: Couldn't load your active connections right now.
connections.manageIdp: Manage them in the identity provider

private.title: Yours alone
private.desc: The space the assistant uses with you that nobody else can reach.
private.lockedBody: Your private scope is owned by you. It is never shown in this console — not to you, not to admins — and is never reachable through the connector. The assistant reads and writes it only on your behalf, from your authenticated session.

signout.title: Sign out
signout.desc: End your console session and log out at your identity provider.

team.idpBanner: Accounts are managed in your identity provider. Create, disable, and role changes here write through to the directory — they are not local to this console. Each member's private memory remains theirs.
team.emptyBody: Adjust the search or role filter.

erase.title: Remove {name}?
erase.body: Their private memory is purged. Shared and global entries they authored are kept, but the authorship is rewritten to the anonymous former-member marker. The identity-provider account is deleted. This is GDPR erasure — not the reversible disable.
erase.typePrompt: Type the member's email to confirm:
erase.typeAria: Type the member's email to confirm erasure

enable.title: Enable {name}?
enable.body: They regain console and API access immediately. Their private memory was never touched.

disable.title: Disable {name}?
disable.body: They lose console and API access immediately. Your identity provider suspends the account; their private memory is untouched and stays theirs.

mute.title: Mute {name}?
mute.body: Shared writes are suspended — on this console and through the assistant. Reading is unaffected, and their private memory stays fully theirs. Reversible anytime.

unmute.title: Unmute {name}?
unmute.body: Shared writes resume immediately — on this console and through the assistant.

inviteDialog.title: Invite a member
inviteDialog.banner: This creates the account in your identity provider and emails an enrolment link. No password is set here. Their private memory remains theirs and is never visible in this console.
inviteDialog.emailHint: Must match the identity provider's directory domain.
inviteDialog.namePlaceholder: Optional — pulled from the IdP if blank
inviteDialog.adminHint: Admins manage scopes, members, and settings. They never see anyone's private memory.
inviteDialog.memberHint: Members read and write shared scopes per the write-scope policy.
inviteDialog.sent: Invite sent via your identity provider

policy.title: Default write scope
policy.desc: Where the assistant writes a new memory when it isn't told which scope to use.
policy.ask_desc: The assistant proposes a scope and the member confirms before anything is written. Safest default for mixed teams.
policy.project_desc: Writes land in whichever project the member is currently working in. Org-wide facts must be promoted manually.
policy.global_desc: Everything defaults to the global scope. Simple, but the shared baseline grows quickly — review regularly.
policy.fallbackLabel: Fallback scope when no project is active
policy.noDefaultHint: No project default set — writes fall back to the global scope until you pick one.

create.title: Who may create scopes
create.desc: Project scopes carve the shared memory into spaces. The global scope is fixed and can't be created or removed.
create.admins_desc: Only admins create, rename, and archive project scopes. Members read and write within them.
create.members_desc: Any member can spin up a project scope. Faster for autonomous teams; expect more scopes to curate.

connector.title: Connector details
connector.desc: The endpoint and client ID your AI client uses to reach shared memory.
connector.descRotate: Rotate the secret if it may have leaked.
connector.noSecret: None — public client (PKCE). Leave the secret field empty in your AI client.
connector.idpBanner: Identity is delegated to your identity provider. Member accounts and passwords are managed under Team, not here.

private.title: Private memory
private.desc: The one setting you can't change — and that's the point.
private.lockedBody: Each member's private memory is owned by them and is never exposed to admins, this console, or the connector. There is no switch to turn this off — it is enforced by the backend, not by configuration.

rotate.title: Rotate the client secret?
rotate.body: The current secret stops working immediately. Any AI client using it must be reconfigured with the new value before it can read or write memory.

toast.rotated: Secret rotated — copy the new value

decision.label: Decision
decision.description: A choice made and the reasoning that fixed it.

convention.label: Convention
convention.description: How the team does a thing — a recurring pattern.

constraint.label: Constraint
constraint.description: A hard rule from outside the team (legal, perf, contract).

open_question.label: Open question
open_question.description: A known unknown, waiting on data or a stakeholder.

glossary.label: Glossary
glossary.description: A term whose meaning the team needs to share.

status.label: Status
status.description: Current state of a thing — short-lived.

entryMenu.protectedHint: This entry is required for Kumbuka to work correctly and can't be deleted.

toast.restoreNyi: Restore not implemented in this build
toast.restoreFailed: Couldn't restore the scope — the server may not support it yet.

archiveConfirm.title: Archive {slug}?
archiveConfirm.body: The scope and its {count} entries become read-only. The assistant stops writing to it. You can restore it later.

private.title: Private
private.titleAttr: Private memories are owned by each user and are not accessible from this console.
private.body: Every member has a private memory scope. It is owned by them and is never exposed to this console — not to admins, and not through the API surface you manage.

syncError.title: Couldn't reach this scope
syncError.body: The backend returned a sync error while loading {slug}. The assistant's last-known copy may be stale.

empty.title: No memories yet
empty.body: This scope is empty. Add the first decision, convention, or constraint — or let the assistant write one as the team works.

noMatch.title: Nothing matches
noMatch.body: No entries in {slug} match your filter. Clear it to see all {count}.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete this memory?
deleteConfirm.body: The assistant will immediately stop recalling it.

writeNote.muted: Shared writes are suspended for your account — reading is unaffected, and your private memory stays yours.
writeNote.global: Default write target for memories the assistant marks org-wide.
writeNote.archived: Read-only — archived scopes can't be written to.
writeNote.project: Writable by admins and members assigned to this project.

scope.idHint: Stable, kebab-case. The assistant addresses the scope by this. Immutable after creation.
scope.banner: New scopes start empty and writable by admins. Adjust who may write in Settings → write-scope policy.
scope.errExists: A scope with this slug already exists — it may be archived. Check the archived scopes.
scope.errForbidden: Only team admins can create or change scopes.

entry.keyHint: Optional stable identifier. Lowercase, dot-namespaced — the assistant looks entries up by this.
entry.keyError: Lowercase letters, digits, dot and hyphen only — no underscores, uppercase, or slashes.
entry.keyExists: A memory with this key already exists in this scope. Choose a different key — it won't be overwritten.
entry.contentPlaceholder: State it plainly, the way you'd want the assistant to recall it.
entry.refHint: Optional link to where this came from. Stored as metadata — never fetched, and no credentials in the URL.

move.title: Move to scope
move.targetHint: The memory keeps its content and author; only its scope changes.
move.noTargets: There's no other scope to move this into yet. Create another scope first.
move.keyHint: A memory with this key already exists in the target scope. Give it a different key to move it without overwriting.
move.keyError: Lowercase letters, numbers, and single . or - separators only.
move.errExists: A memory with that key already exists in the target scope. Choose a different key — nothing was overwritten.
move.errForbidden: Only admins can move memories between scopes.
move.failed: Couldn't move this memory. Please try again.

entryError.muted: Your account is muted — shared-scope writes are suspended. Ask a team admin to lift the mute.
entryError.protected: This entry is protected and can't be changed.
entryError.exists: A memory with this key already exists in this scope. Choose a different key — the existing one won't be overwritten.
entryError.stale: This entry changed since you opened it — reload and try again.
entryError.generic: Couldn't save this entry. Please try again.

explain.title: How kumbuka works
explain.mnemonicsLead: A mnemonic is one durable fact your team has agreed on — a decision, a convention, a hard constraint. Each is typed, so an assistant knows how to weigh it.
explain.flowNote: Every read is scoped; every write is attributed and reviewable. Nothing reaches an assistant that the team hasn't curated.
explain.scopesLead: Memory is organised into scopes. An assistant reads the global baseline first, then the project it is working in.
explain.globalDesc: One organization-wide baseline every project inherits.
explain.projectDesc: Per-project memory — one scope per codebase or product.
explain.privateBody: Every member also has a private scope the assistant uses for them alone. It is backend-enforced never to appear in this console — not here, not to admins, not through the connector.

invite.title: Invite your team
invite.label: Email addresses
invite.hint: One per line. Invalid lines are flagged below — valid ones go out through the existing invite flow.
invite.send: {count, plural, =0 {Send invites} one {Send # invite} other {Send # invites}}
invite.sentHead: {count, plural, one {# invite} other {# invites}} sent via your identity provider
invite.sentNote: Each receives a set-up-credentials link — no password is set here. You can invite more from Team & users any time.
invite.sentToast: {count, plural, =0 {No invites sent} one {# invite sent} other {# invites sent}}

scopes.title: Set up your scopes
scopes.lead: Set up the project scopes your team will use. Each gets a stable <m>kebab-slug</m> id the assistant addresses it by — the id is fixed once created.
scopes.nameHint: Type a name; the id is derived automatically.
scopes.globalNote: The <m>global</m> scope already exists and applies to every project — you don't create it here.
scopes.createdToast: {count, plural, one {# scope} other {# scopes}} created
scopes.createFailed: {count, plural, one {# scope} other {# scopes}} couldn't be created
